name: AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer – Associate (SOA-C03)
description: Associate-level certification (formerly SysOps Administrator) for cloud operations engineers deploying, managing, and operating workloads on AWS. Validates ability to monitor, remediate, ensure reliability, automate provisioning, and secure AWS environments. 1+ years hands-on experience. 65 questions (50 scored + 15 unscored), 130 minutes, 720/1000 passing score.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document is a collection of practice exam questions for the **AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer – Associate (SOA-C03)** certification. The questions were selected because they represent realistic operational scenarios commonly encountered when managing and operating workloads on AWS.

### Certification context

This certification validates the ability to deploy, manage, and operate scalable systems on AWS, including topics such as:

* Implementing monitoring, logging, and automated remediation (CloudWatch, EventBridge)
* Designing for high availability, scalability, and disaster recovery
* Automating infrastructure provisioning (CloudFormation, CDK, Systems Manager)
* Implementing security controls and compliance policies
* Configuring networking and content delivery (VPC, Route 53, CloudFront)

### About the questions

Each question in this document is independent and should be analyzed as a standalone scenario. Practice exams intentionally present isolated situations, and questions may appear in any order during the actual certification exam. Information from one question should never be assumed to apply to another.

For each scenario, this document includes:

* The original practice exam question.
* An explanation of the operational reasoning behind the correct answer.
* A discussion of why the remaining options are less appropriate for the stated requirements.

### Learning objective

The primary goal of this material is to develop operational reasoning rather than memorization. The focus should be on understanding the monitoring, automation, and reliability requirements presented in each scenario, identifying the AWS services and operational patterns that best satisfy those requirements, and recognizing the trade-offs involved.

By studying the decision-making process behind each answer, readers can build the operational skills expected for the AWS certification exam.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer – Associate (SOA-C03)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer – Associate (SOA-C03)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Monitoring, Logging, Analysis, Remediation, and Performance Optimization
domains.description: Domain 1 (22%): Implement metrics, alarms, and filters using CloudWatch. Collect and analyze logs. Remediate issues based on monitoring data. Implement performance optimization strategies.

domains.name: Reliability and Business Continuity
domains.description: Domain 2 (22%): Implement scalability and elasticity, design highly available and resilient environments, implement backup and restore strategies.

domains.name: Deployment, Provisioning, and Automation
domains.description: Domain 3 (22%): Provision and maintain cloud resources (CloudFormation, CDK, Service Catalog). Automate manual or repeatable processes. Manage multi-account, multi-Region architectures.

domains.name: Security and Compliance
domains.description: Domain 4 (16%): Implement and manage security and compliance policies. Implement data and infrastructure protection strategies.

domains.name: Networking and Content Delivery
domains.description: Domain 5 (18%): Implement networking features and connectivity (VPC, Direct Connect, VPN). Configure domains, DNS, and content delivery.
